mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market and improving consumer confidence, which typically translates into positive sentiment for the broader equity market over the short-to-medium term.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window, or the correlation is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at or near the zero lower bound, it signals an extremely accommodative monetary policy stance, which encourages risk-taking and drives positive S&P 500 returns as investors seek yield outside of safe assets.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the average return is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates broad-based economic recovery and robust demand, signaling strong corporate earnings prospects and leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive 3-month growth, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, a significant month-over-month drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a turning point in the labor market, reducing recession fears and providing a short-term boost to equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is above 7% and drops by more than 0.2pp, SPX 5-day forward returns are negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive month-over-month growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects underlying economic strength, consumer confidence, and investment in the real estate sector, which translates into positive sentiment for the broader equity market.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST month-over-month growth and SPX 60-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth) alongside a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an environment where the Federal Reserve has ample room for accommodative policy, which is generally supportive of equity markets.
failure_condition: In periods meeting the trigger conditions, SPX 20-day returns are not statistically different from zero or are frequently negative (more than 50% of observations).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction or slow growth, an acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a potential turning point and robust recovery, leading to increased investor optimism and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% in a slow growth regime, or the average return is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold indicates significant slack in the labor market, which dampens consumer spending, reduces corporate profits, and signals ongoing economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are predominantly positive (more than 60% of observations) when UNRATE is above 7.0%, or the average return is not statistically negative.
